title: Create access token to onboard collector to Grafana Fleet Management

blocks.content:
If your Alloy collector isn't shown on the Fleet Management page, you can manually register it.

In this milestone, you generate an access token that registers your collector with Fleet Management. In the next milestone, you add this token as the `password` in your Alloy configuration file.
To create an access token, complete the following steps:
Use the tool on this page to create a new token. Give the token a name and click **Create token**.
Copy the token and save it in a secure password file. You need the token for the next step, and you can't view it again after you leave the page.

The generated token comes with the predefined scope `set:alloy-data-write`, which includes scopes for reading remote configurations in Fleet Management and writing telemetry to Grafana Cloud databases.

In the next milestone, you use the token to register the collector with Fleet Management.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your journey, you can explore the following paths:

- [Onboard collectors deployed in Kubernetes to Fleet Management](/docs/grafana-cloud/send-data/fleet-management/set-up/onboard-collectors/kubernetes/)
- [Grafana Cloud Access Policies](/docs/grafana-cloud/security-and-account-management/authentication-and-permissions/access-policies/)
- [Create tokens for an access policy](/docs/grafana-cloud/security-and-account-management/authentication-and-permissions/access-policies/create-access-policies/#create-tokens-for-an-access-policy)
